file.content:
name: linux

on: [ push, pull_request ]

env:
  UBUNTU_DEP_BUILD: |
    gcc g++ pkg-config systemd libevdev-dev libglib2.0-dev
    libjson-glib-dev libsystemd-dev libudev-dev libunistring-dev
    python3-dev python3-evdev swig python3-sphinx
  UBUNTU_DEP_TEST: check python3-gi python3-lxml valgrind
  PIP_PACKAGES: meson ninja libevdev pyudev pytest sphinx_rtd_theme black==25.12.0 ruff==0.15.12

jobs:
  build-and-dist:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - name: default
            meson_args: ''
          - name: plain
            meson_args: '-Dbuildtype=plain'
          - name: release
            meson_args: '-Dbuildtype=release'
          - name: documentation
            meson_args: '-Ddocumentation=true'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/pkginstall
        with:
          apt: $UBUNTU_DEP_BUILD $UBUNTU_DEP_TEST
          pip: $PIP_PACKAGES
      - name: meson test ${{matrix.meson_args}}
        uses: ./.github/actions/meson
        with:
          meson_args: --prefix=$PWD/_instdir ${{matrix.meson_args}}
          ninja_args: install
      - name: check installation of data files
        run:  diff -u <(cd data/devices; ls *.device) <(cd _instdir/share/libratbag; ls *.device)
      - name: ninja uninstall
        uses: ./.github/actions/meson
        with:
          meson_args: --prefix=$PWD/_instdir
          ninja_args: uninstall
      - name: check if any files are left after uninstall
        run: (test -d _instdir && tree _instdir && exit 1) || exit 0
      # Capture all the meson logs, even if we failed
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: ${{ always() }}  # even if we fail
        with:
          name: 'meson test logs ${{ matrix.name }}'
          path: |
            builddir/meson-logs/testlog*.txt
            builddir/meson-logs/meson-log.txt

file.content:
# A Github Action to build a repository with meson
name: "Setup and run meson"
description: "Setup and run meson"
inputs:
  builddir:
    description: "The build directory"
    default: "builddir"
  srcdir:
    description: "The source directory"
    default: "."
  meson_args:
    description: "Arguments passed to meson setup"
    default: ""
  meson_skip_test:
    description: "Set to a nonempty string to skip meson test"
    default: ""
  meson_test_args:
    description: "Arguments passed to meson test"
    default: ""
  meson_precmd:
    description: "Set to 'sudo' to run with sudo"
    default: ""
  ninja_args:
    description: "Arguments passed to ninja"
    default: ""
  ninja_precmd:
    description: "Set to 'sudo' to run with sudo"
    default: ""
runs:
  using: "composite"
  steps:
    - run: |
        ${{inputs.meson_precmd}} meson setup ${{inputs.builddir}} ${{inputs.srcdir}} ${{inputs.meson_args}}
        ${{inputs.meson_precmd}} meson configure ${{inputs.builddir}}
        ${{inputs.ninja_precmd}} ninja -C ${{inputs.builddir}} ${{inputs.ninja_args}}
        if [[ -z "${{inputs.meson_skip_test}}" ]]; then
          ${{inputs.meson_precmd}} meson test -C ${{inputs.builddir}} --print-errorlogs ${{inputs.meson_test_args}};
        fi
      shell: bash

FROM ubuntu:24.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Update and install system dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -yq --no-install-suggests --no-install-recommends \
    gcc g++ pkg-config systemd libevdev-dev libglib2.0-dev \
    libjson-glib-dev libsystemd-dev libudev-dev libunistring-dev \
    python3-dev python3-evdev swig python3-sphinx \
    check python3-gi python3-lxml valgrind \
    git curl wget tree python3-pip

# Upgrade pip
RUN python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip

# Install pip packages
RUN python3 -m pip install --upgrade \
    meson ninja libevdev pyudev pytest sphinx_rtd_theme black==25.12.0 ruff==0.15.12

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the repository
COPY repo/ /app/

# Copy the run script
COPY run.sh /run.sh
RUN chmod +x /run.sh

ENTRYPOINT ["/run.sh"]

content.thinking:
The log ends right after "=== Checking if any files are left after uninstall ===" with a newline. The script didn't complete and print "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS". 

There might be an issue with the last step in the run.sh. Let me re-examine it. The command was:

`(test -d _instdir && tree _instdir && exit 1) || exit 0`

But wait - when this command succeeds (i.e., _instdir doesn't exist), it should print "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS". But we're not seeing that printed.

Let me re-examine the run.sh to see if maybe the script is exiting early or if there's an issue with the bash script.

Actually, looking at the run.sh I wrote:

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

cd /app

# ... steps ...

# Step 4: Check if any files are left after uninstall
echo "=== Checking if any files are left after uninstall ==="
(test -d _instdir && tree _instdir && exit 1) || exit 0

echo "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS"
```

So after the `(test -d _instdir && tree _instdir && exit 1) || exit 0` command, it should print "FINAL_STATUS = SUCCESS".

But the script seems to be stopping after that command. Let me think about what's happening:

1. The `_instdir` directory doesn't exist after uninstall (which is correct)
2. So `test -d _instdir` returns false
3. The && chain stops, so the exit 1 is never reached
4. The parenthesized expression returns 1 (the exit status of test -d)
5. So the || is triggered and `exit 0` is executed
6. This should execute, which means it exits with status 0

But wait - `exit 0` in the context of the || actually exits the shell! It doesn't just set the exit code. 

The script is terminating at that point and never reaches the next echo statement. I need to restructure the run.sh to avoid using `exit` in a way that prematurely terminates the entire script.
